summary: To determine interstitial fluid volume, one must use indirect measurement methods like those employing indicators distributed via blood circulation; among options considered, radioactively tagged water combined with radiolabled albumin is optimal because these agents effectively track overall fluid compartments precisely. This approach ensures accurate estimation despite practical limitations against direct volumetric assessment. Agent2 concluded based on available data that measuring interstitial fluid volume requires indirect methods, specifically endorsing combination therapy utilizing radioactive water paired with radiolabeled albumin for precise estimations owing to their comprehensive tissue coverage properties.
